title: fix: preaggregate KPI activations
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds a user_first_activity_mv aggregate maintained from generation_event.
- Rewrites kpi_activations to read minMerge(first_activity_time) instead of grouping raw generation_event per dashboard request.
- Reduces the 15GB-per-call activation scan that was timing out in Tinybird.

Validation:
- npx biome check --write apps/model-monitor/src/hooks/useModelMonitor.js apps/model-monitor/src/App.jsx gen.pollinations.ai/src/routes/model-status.ts packages/polli-cli/src/commands/stats.ts gen.pollinations.ai/src/docs/public-stats.md
- npm run typecheck --prefix gen.pollinations.ai
- npm run build --prefix packages/polli-cli
- npm run build --prefix apps/model-monitor

Note: no Tinybird deploy was performed. A dry-run validation was attempted earlier and was blocked by unrelated workspace drift where remote Tinybird objects are absent from this checkout.

gist.headline: Activations stop eating 15GB
gist.blurb: The activation dashboard was rereading the whole meadow for every question. Now it keeps a tidy first-activity ledger, so the numbers arrive before the flowers wilt.
gist.summary: Added a `user_first_activity_mv` aggregate maintained from `generation_event`, and rewrote `kpi_activations` to use `minMerge(first_activity_time)` instead of grouping raw events on every dashboard request.
gist.impact: Activation KPI requests avoid the previous 15GB raw-event scan per call, making the dashboard substantially more reliable and less prone to Tinybird timeouts.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Polly as a careful garden keeper using a small clipboard to record each code-plant's first bloom in a compact ledger, while a nearby CRT monitor robot happily reads a fast, tidy activation chart instead of digging through an enormous pile of event scrolls. The first-bloom ledger represents the materialized first-activity aggregate and the calm dashboard represents the faster KPI query.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny gardener visor, recording the first bloom of lime green (#ecf874) code-plants in a neat glowing ledger while a chunky CRT monitor robot displays a clean rising activation chart; a huge dusty pile of old event scrolls rests quietly in the background. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel mint-lavender-peach gradients, warm lighting, subtle CRT glow, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, emotionally warm and inviting.
